text.title: gcp-arch-patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for gcp-arch-patterns as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Single-zone GKE clusters for production workloads.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A zone outage takes the entire cluster offline — control plane and all worker nodes. Regional clusters survive zone failures with automatic rescheduling.

failure-mode.description: Cloud SQL with `availability_type = ZONAL` in production.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: ZONAL provides no automatic failover. A zone outage requires manual promotion of a replica, causing 5–30 minutes of downtime depending on replication lag.

failure-mode.description: Pub/Sub subscription without a dead-letter topic for production workloads.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A single bad message can block an entire subscription if there is no DLT to absorb it after max retries. The subscription falls arbitrarily behind with no alerting mechanism.
